mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound, sustained policy accommodation lowers the discount rate for future earnings and forces investors into riskier assets, driving equity prices higher over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC ZIRP environment established in late 2008
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically higher when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% compared to periods when it is > 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the rate of increase of the unemployment rate signals that the peak of labor market stress has passed, leading to a forward-looking rally in equities as the worst recession fears recede.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose from 4.7% in late 2007 to 9.5% by mid-2009
failure_condition: The 3-month change in the 3-month change of UNRATE has a positive correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: After a collapse, housing starts stabilizing and turning positive from extremely low levels signals a bottom in a key cyclical sector, leading to improved investor sentiment and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market collapse was central to the GFC
failure_condition: Positive 3-month changes in HOUST when the level is below 600k units are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of contracting industrial production and falling inflation signals a severe aggregate demand shortfall. This increases the probability of sustained, aggressive monetary easing, which boosts equity valuations through lower discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:GFC was a major demand shock
failure_condition: Periods where both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL are contracting year-over-year are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: Negative monthly changes in nonfarm payrolls directly reflect a contraction in economic activity and labor income, leading to lower near-term corporate earnings expectations and negative pressure on equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Massive job losses throughout 2008 and H1 2009
failure_condition: The correlation between the monthly change in PAYEMS and the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year contraction in retail sales indicates a significant pullback in consumer spending. This directly implies lower corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer confidence collapsed during the GFC
failure_condition: Periods following a negative year-over-year RSAFS print do not exhibit statistically significant negative 20-day S&P 500 returns compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising inflation and contracting real GDP creates a stagflationary environment. This constrains the central bank's ability to provide monetary stimulus, increasing uncertainty and risk premia, which is negative for equities.
failure_condition: Periods with rising CPI and falling GDP are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth confirms a cyclical upturn in the goods-producing sector, leading to upward revisions in earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns, especially during a recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a highly cyclical series
failure_condition: The 3-month change in the 3-month change of INDPRO does not have a statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns during NBER-dated recovery periods.
